title: RATs: Playful Agentic Robot Learning
summary: RATs (Robotics Agent Teams) studies Playful Agentic Robot Learning, where an embodied coding agent uses self-directed play as a continual skill-learning stage before downstream tasks arrive. During play, RATs proposes novel yet learnable exploratory tasks, plans and executes robot-code policies, verifies intermediate progress, diagnoses failures and retries with dense step-level feedback, and distills successful executions into a persistent code skill library that the agent later reuses to solve new tasks. On LIBERO-PRO and MolmoSpaces, play-learned skills improve held-out downstream tasks over no-play and random-play baselines, with 20.6 and 17.0 percentage-point gains over CaP-Agent0; the learned skills also plug into other inference-time Code-as-Policy agents, improving RoboSuite and real-world transfer by 8.9 and 8.8 points without finetuning the underlying model.
